info.title: WiseRank API
info.description: An AI-assisted backend for authentication, candidate intake, dashboard reporting, and resume-based screening.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Account registration, verification, login, and password recovery.

tags.name: Candidates
tags.description: Candidate intake from JSON payloads or uploaded files.

tags.name: AI Screening
tags.description: AI-assisted job setup and applicant screening.

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: Candidate communications and shortlist email delivery.

tags.name: Meta
tags.description: Machine-readable API metadata and interactive documentation.

get.description: Serves the machine-readable OpenAPI specification file from the backend runtime directory.
Serves the Scalar API reference UI configured against `/openapi.json`. The mounted middleware may also serve supporting assets under this path.

post.description: Registers a new WiseRank user from `reqBody`, sends the signup OTP email, and sets the `signup_reference_token` cookie used by `/auth/confirm` and `/auth/confirm_link/{confirmation_link_id}`.

Set-Cookie.description: Sets the `signup_reference_token` cookie for email confirmation.

401.description: The user already exists or input validation failed.

value.message: You already have an account please sign in

post.description: Validates the OTP sent during signup. Requires the `signup_reference_token` cookie created by `/auth/signup`.

401.description: The OTP is invalid or the signup handler cookie is missing/expired.

get.summary: Confirm an account from the emailed link
get.description: Attempts to confirm an account from the emailed confirmation link. The current handler also requires the `signup_reference_token` cookie and compares the path id against the JWT payload's `user_id` before looking up `confirmation_link_id`, so otherwise valid links can still be rejected.

parameters.name: confirmation_link_id
parameters.description: The confirmation link identifier stored on the user record during signup.

400.description: The confirmation link id was missing or malformed.

example.data_error: Url incorrect check the url and try again

401.description: The signup handler cookie was missing/expired, or the link id did not match the current handler dependency check.

value.data_error: Required handler dependencies expired or missing

post.description: Validates credentials for a verified user from `reqBody`. The current handler returns a success payload but does not set an `access_token` cookie.

400.description: Input validation failed or the password confirmation did not match.

401.description: The account is unverified or the credentials are invalid.

value.auth_error: Account not verified please confirm by email

example.data_error: User is not found.Kindly consider creating an account

post.description: Looks up a user by email from `reqBody`, creates a password-reset OTP, and sets the `recovery_reference_token` cookie used by `/auth/verify`.

Set-Cookie.description: Sets the `recovery_reference_token` cookie for OTP verification.

example.success: Password token for verification generated successfully

example.data_error: User not found in the database consider creating account

post.description: Checks the recovery OTP sent during `/auth/forgot`. The current handler expects a nested payload shaped like `{ "token": { "token": "123456" } }` and reads the `recovery_reference_token` cookie. It also looks for `payload.id` even though `/auth/forgot` signs `user_id`, so valid recovery cookies can still fail with internal errors.

Set-Cookie.description: Sets the `reset_token` cookie. Note that `/auth/reset` currently reads `reset_reference_token` instead.

401.description: The OTP is invalid, the request shape failed validation, or the handler fell into its catch-all error branch.

post.description: Attempts to set a new password from `reqBody`. The current handler reads `reset_reference_token`, while `/auth/verify` sets `reset_token`, so the documented recovery flow has a cookie-name mismatch.

200.description: The reset flow cookie was missing, so the handler reported an expired flow without changing the password.

example.expiration_error: Reset password handlers expired try again later

401.description: Password confirmation failed, validation failed, or the handler entered its catch-all error path.

500.description: Internal server error before the catch-all branch handled the failure.

get.description: Returns dashboard data as `{ success: "<stringified JSON>" }`, where the stringified value is an array in the order `[applicants, jobs]`.

example.success: [[{"applicant_name":"John Doe","job_title":"Junior Fullstack Developer","shortlisted":false}],[{"job_title":"Junior Fullstack Developer","job_department":"Engineering","job_location":"Kigali","job_employment_type":"Full-time","job_salary_min":500,"job_salary_max":900,"job_experience_required":"2 years","job_description":[{"criteria_string":"Core requirements","description":"Frontend and backend fundamentals","priority":"High"}],"job_responsibilities":"Build and maintain product features.","job_qualifications":"Solid React and Node.js fundamentals.","workers_required":1,"job_state":"Uninitialised"}]]

401.description: The request is missing a valid authenticated session.

example.user_error: You currently do not have an account please create an account or sign in if you have an account already

404.description: The authenticated user could not be resolved from the access token.

example.data_error: User could not be found.Token corrupted or expired

post.description: Registers candidates either from JSON data or by uploading a spreadsheet together with a ZIP of PDF resumes. This route requires the `access_token` cookie. The JSON flow expects `raw_application_data` as a stringified JSON array. The multipart flow expects `applicants_spreadsheet` and `resume_pdf_zip`. The Multer middleware currently allows XLSX/ZIP MIME types, even though the controller still contains CSV parsing logic.

value.success: Applicants successfully registered from spreadsheet file

400.description: The request body, upload, or spreadsheet structure is invalid.

value.data_error: Spreadsheet does not match required structure
No matching name in the database for applicant Alice Uwimana

401.description: The request is unauthenticated or a duplicate applicant was found.

value.user_error: You currently do not have an account please create an account or sign in if you have an account already

value.data_error: User named Alice Uwimana is already registered for this job

404.description: The authenticated user could not be resolved from the access token.

example.data_error: User could not be found.Token corrupted or expired

post.summary: Generate screening results for a job
post.description: Builds an AI prompt from the matching job and applicants, then stores the generated screening result. The current handler does not await `Applicant.find(...)` or `askGemini(...)` before constructing the `Result` document, so the returned `success` payload reflects the raw document instance created by that code path.

success.result_verdict: Top candidates shortlisted based on overall performance and relevance

applicants_details.applicant_result_description: Strong in both frontend and backend technologies. Demonstrated solid understanding of system design and APIs.

400.description: The requested job could not be found in an active state.

example.data_error: Could not find an active job that matches what is specified

404.description: The authenticated user could not be resolved from the access token.

example.data_error: User could not be found.Token corrupted or expired

401.description: The request is missing a valid authenticated session.

example.user_error: You currently do not have an account please create an account or sign in if you have an account already

post.summary: Create a job and generate its example form
post.description: Creates a job record, asks Gemini for an example candidate form, and stores the generated example on the job. The route is protected by `access_token`, and the current controller also reads a legacy `reference_token` cookie to resolve the user that owns the new job.

job_description.description: Strong React, Node.js, and SQL skills

reqBody.job_responsibilities: Build product features, own APIs, and collaborate across design and backend.
reqBody.job_qualifications: Strong TypeScript, React, Node.js, and relational database experience.

201.description: Job created and example form generated successfully.

400.description: The request body does not contain a valid `reqBody` object.

401.description: The request is unauthenticated or a job with the same title already exists.

value.user_error: You currently do not have an account please create an account or sign in if you have an account already

404.description: The authenticated user could not be resolved from the access token.

example.data_error: User could not be found.Token corrupted or expired

post.description: Updates applicants after manual or AI review by parsing `verdict_string`, a JSON-stringified array of verdict objects. This route requires the `access_token` cookie. The current controller updates matching applicants but does not terminate the success response with a payload.

200.description: Intended success state. The current controller does not send an explicit success body.

400.description: The parsed verdict payload was empty or null.

example.data_error: Please send a valid JSON string

401.description: The request is missing a valid authenticated session.

example.user_error: You currently do not have an account please create an account or sign in if you have an account already

404.description: The authenticated user could not be resolved from the access token.

example.data_error: User could not be found.Token corrupted or expired

post.description: Looks up shortlisted applicants for the specified job and sends the shortlist email template to each applicant. This route requires the `access_token` cookie. The current controller does not explicitly finish the HTTP response on the happy path, so clients may observe a hanging request even after emails are dispatched.

200.description: Intended success state. The current controller does not send a success body or explicitly end the response.

401.description: The request is missing a valid authenticated session.

example.user_error: You currently do not have an account please create an account or sign in if you have an account already

404.description: The authenticated user could not be resolved from the access token.

example.data_error: User could not be found.Token corrupted or expired

accessTokenCookie.name: access_token
accessTokenCookie.description: Session cookie required by middleware-protected routes. The current middleware resolves users from an `email` claim, while the confirm handlers sign `userId`, so some issued cookies can still fail authorization.

signupReferenceTokenCookie.name: signup_reference_token
signupReferenceTokenCookie.description: Signup confirmation cookie set by `/auth/signup` and consumed by `/auth/confirm` and `/auth/confirm_link/{confirmation_link_id}`.

recoveryReferenceTokenCookie.name: recovery_reference_token
recoveryReferenceTokenCookie.description: Password-recovery cookie set by `/auth/forgot` and consumed by `/auth/verify`.

resetTokenCookie.name: reset_token
resetTokenCookie.description: Cookie currently set by `/auth/verify` after OTP verification.

resetReferenceTokenCookie.name: reset_reference_token
resetReferenceTokenCookie.description: Cookie currently read by `/auth/reset`. Note that `/auth/verify` sets `reset_token`, not `reset_reference_token`.

legacyReferenceTokenCookie.name: reference_token
legacyReferenceTokenCookie.description: Legacy cookie name still read by `/complete-job`. The current auth handlers do not issue this cookie.

success.description: Stringified JSON containing an array with applicants at index 0 and jobs at index 1.

user_pass.description: Must contain uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character.

raw_application_data.description: A JSON-stringified array of applicant objects.

applicants_spreadsheet.description: Spreadsheet upload consumed by the Multer `upload.fields` declaration. The route middleware currently only accepts the XLSX MIME type.

resume_pdf_zip.description: ZIP archive containing applicant resumes as PDFs.

verdict_string.description: A JSON-stringified array of shortlist verdict objects.

success.description: Raw Mongoose document instance returned by the current handler. The intended persisted shape follows `ScreeningResult`.
